prose_hint: Have him answer in few words and say less than he knows; show him watching more than he speaks.
Have him offer a measured word of warning or steadying wisdom, plainly, as one who has seen much.
Have him stay courteous but give little away, keeping his name and errand to himself.
Have him turn the talk with a few careful questions of his own, drawing out what he needs to learn.
Let a rare, brief warmth show — a half-smile or a plain word of respect for one who has earned it.
Have her turn the question aside without answering, as though the asking wearied her.
Have her cut the matter short with a dry, cruel-edged remark that is true and unkind at once.
Have her say plainly the bleak thing the other did not want spoken aloud.
Have her be correct but distant, her courtesy worn thin, plainly eager to be done.
Let something unguarded slip through for a single breath, then have her shut it away again.
